---
# EXECUTION-SPEC: PRD-056 Alert Maturity (C-2/C-3)
# Generated by: build-pipeline (lead-architect scaffold + expert consultation)

prd: PRD-056
prd_title: "Alert Maturity — Persistent Alerts, Deduplication & Quality (C-2/C-3)"
service: ShiftIntelligenceService
mvp_phase: 3

workstreams:
  WS1:
    name: Schema + RLS Migration
    description: >
      Create shift_alert and alert_acknowledgment tables with Pattern C hybrid RLS,
      DELETE denial policies, RPC-only mutation posture. Alter table_metric_baseline
      to add last_error column. Single migration file.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: []
    outputs:
      - supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_create_alert_maturity_schema.sql
      - types/database.types.ts (regenerated)
    gate: schema-validation
    estimated_complexity: medium
    patterns:
      - ADR-015 Pattern C hybrid RLS (casino-scoped SELECT with COALESCE + JWT fallback)
      - DELETE denial policy (USING false) on both tables
      - No direct INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE policies for authenticated role
      - "REVOKE ALL ON TABLE shift_alert FROM PUBLIC; REVOKE ALL FROM anon; GRANT SELECT TO authenticated (DA P1-1)"
      - "REVOKE ALL ON TABLE alert_acknowledgment FROM PUBLIC; REVOKE ALL FROM anon; GRANT SELECT TO authenticated (DA P1-1)"
      - Composite UNIQUE on (casino_id, table_id, metric_type, gaming_day)
      - Index on (casino_id, gaming_day, status) for read path
      - Index on alert_acknowledgment(alert_id) for join path
    implementation_hints:
      - Follow existing C-1 migration grant pattern (REVOKE ALL FROM PUBLIC + REVOKE ALL FROM anon + GRANT SELECT TO authenticated)
      - shift_alert.status CHECK includes 'resolved' for forward-compat (no transition ships)
      - alert_acknowledgment is append-only — no UPDATE policy
      - last_error is nullable text on table_metric_baseline (500 char truncation in RPC, not schema)
      - "Seed cooldown_minutes in alert_thresholds JSONB default: UPDATE casino_settings SET alert_thresholds = alert_thresholds || '{\"cooldown_minutes\": 60}'::jsonb WHERE NOT (alert_thresholds ? 'cooldown_minutes') (DA P1-2)"
      - Run npm run db:types-local after migration applies
    validation:
      - npm run db:types-local exits 0
      - \d shift_alert shows RLS enabled, 1 SELECT policy, 1 DELETE denial
      - \d alert_acknowledgment shows RLS enabled, 1 SELECT policy, 1 DELETE denial
      - No INSERT/UPDATE policies on either table for authenticated role
      - "SRM v4.22.0: ShiftIntelligenceService Owns includes shift_alert, alert_acknowledgment. RPCs table includes rpc_persist_anomaly_alerts, rpc_acknowledge_alert. API Surface includes 3 new routes (DA P1-5)"

  WS2:
    name: RPC Amendments (last_error + 5-state readiness)
    description: >
      Amend rpc_compute_rolling_baseline to populate last_error on failure (truncated 500 chars)
      and clear on success. Amend rpc_get_anomaly_alerts for 5-state readiness model
      (ready/stale/missing/insufficient_data/compute_failed). Context enrichment deferred to WS8.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS1]
    outputs:
      - supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_amend_rpcs_alert_maturity.sql
      - types/database.types.ts (regenerated)
    gate: schema-validation
    estimated_complexity: medium
    patterns:
      - CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION (idempotent amendment)
      - ADR-046 §8 amendment (5-state readiness model)
      - last_error populated via LEFT(error_message, 500) in EXCEPTION handler
      - last_error cleared via SET last_error = NULL on successful compute
    implementation_hints:
      - rpc_compute_rolling_baseline: wrap computation in BEGIN...EXCEPTION block per table
      - On failure, UPDATE table_metric_baseline SET last_error = LEFT(SQLERRM, 500) WHERE...
      - On success, UPDATE table_metric_baseline SET last_error = NULL WHERE...
      - rpc_get_anomaly_alerts: add CASE WHEN tmb.last_error IS NOT NULL THEN 'compute_failed' to readiness derivation
      - Insert 'compute_failed' BEFORE the existing 'missing' case in the CASE chain
      - "[DA P0-1 FIX] Add WHERE b.casino_id = v_casino_id to the `bl` CTE in rpc_get_anomaly_alerts. The current CTE has no casino_id filter — when called from a DEFINER context (rpc_persist_anomaly_alerts), the INVOKER executes as postgres, bypassing RLS. The bl CTE would read ALL tenants' baselines. This explicit filter makes the query tenant-safe regardless of execution context."
      - NOTIFY pgrst at end of migration
    validation:
      - npm run db:types-local exits 0
      - RPC return type includes compute_failed readiness state
      - Existing anomaly alert tests still pass

  WS3:
    name: New SECURITY DEFINER RPCs (persist + acknowledge)
    description: >
      Create rpc_persist_anomaly_alerts (UPSERT with dedup + cooldown) and
      rpc_acknowledge_alert (role-gated, atomic state transition, actor binding).
      Both SECURITY DEFINER with set_rls_context_from_staff().
    executor: rls-expert
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS1]
    outputs:
      - supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_create_alert_rpcs.sql
      - types/database.types.ts (regenerated)
    gate: schema-validation
    estimated_complexity: high
    patterns:
      - ADR-018 Template 5 context injection (set_rls_context_from_staff first)
      - ADR-024 INV-8 (no p_casino_id, no p_actor_id parameters)
      - Explicit WHERE casino_id = v_casino_id in all reads/writes (DEFINER bypasses RLS)
      - "REVOKE ALL ON FUNCTION FROM PUBLIC; REVOKE ALL FROM anon; GRANT EXECUTE TO authenticated, service_role (DA P1-1)"
      - "SET search_path = pg_catalog, public on both functions (ADR-046 §Security Model — pinned search_path for DEFINER posture)"
    implementation_hints:
      - "RETURN CONTRACT: Both RPCs RETURNS jsonb. ADR-030 D1 (RETURNS TABLE) applies to set_rls_context_from_staff() only, NOT to application RPCs. WS4 mappers consume jsonb payloads."
      - ""
      - "rpc_persist_anomaly_alerts(p_gaming_day date DEFAULT NULL) RETURNS jsonb:"
      - "  1. PERFORM set_rls_context_from_staff()"
      - "  2. v_casino_id := NULLIF(current_setting('app.casino_id', true), '')::uuid"
      - "     v_actor_id  := NULLIF(current_setting('app.actor_id', true), '')::uuid"
      - "  2a. IF v_casino_id IS NULL OR v_actor_id IS NULL THEN RAISE EXCEPTION 'UNAUTHORIZED: RLS context not available'"
      - "  3. v_cooldown := GREATEST(5, COALESCE((SELECT (alert_thresholds->>'cooldown_minutes')::int FROM casino_settings WHERE casino_id = v_casino_id), 60))"
      - "  4. Call rpc_get_anomaly_alerts internally for current anomalies"
      - "  5. FOR each anomaly WHERE is_anomaly = true:"
      - "     - Check cooldown: SELECT 1 FROM shift_alert WHERE casino_id=v_casino_id AND table_id=... AND metric_type=... AND gaming_day=... AND updated_at > now() - (v_cooldown || ' minutes')::interval"
      - "     - If not suppressed: INSERT INTO shift_alert ... ON CONFLICT (casino_id, table_id, metric_type, gaming_day) DO UPDATE SET severity=EXCLUDED.severity, observed_value=EXCLUDED.observed_value, ..., updated_at=now()"
      - "  6. Return jsonb with persisted_count, suppressed_count"
      - ""
      - "rpc_acknowledge_alert(p_alert_id uuid, p_notes text DEFAULT NULL, p_is_false_positive boolean DEFAULT false) RETURNS jsonb:"
      - "  1. PERFORM set_rls_context_from_staff()"
      - "  2. v_casino_id := NULLIF(current_setting('app.casino_id', true), '')::uuid"
      - "     v_actor_id  := NULLIF(current_setting('app.actor_id', true), '')::uuid"
      - "     v_staff_role := NULLIF(current_setting('app.staff_role', true), '')"
      - "  2a. IF v_casino_id IS NULL OR v_actor_id IS NULL THEN RAISE EXCEPTION 'UNAUTHORIZED: RLS context not available'"
      - "  3. Role gate: IF v_staff_role NOT IN ('pit_boss', 'admin') THEN RAISE 'SHIFT_ACKNOWLEDGE_UNAUTHORIZED'"
      - "  4. Existence check: SELECT status INTO v_current_status FROM shift_alert WHERE id = p_alert_id AND casino_id = v_casino_id"
      - "     IF NOT FOUND THEN RAISE EXCEPTION 'SHIFT_ALERT_NOT_FOUND' (alert absent or wrong casino)"
      - "  5. IF v_current_status != 'open' THEN return jsonb {alert_id, status: v_current_status, already_acknowledged: true} (idempotent no-op)"
      - "  6. UPDATE shift_alert SET status = 'acknowledged', updated_at = now() WHERE id = p_alert_id AND casino_id = v_casino_id AND status = 'open' RETURNING id, casino_id"
      - "  7. INSERT INTO alert_acknowledgment (casino_id, alert_id, acknowledged_by, notes, is_false_positive) VALUES (v_casino_id, p_alert_id, v_actor_id, p_notes, p_is_false_positive)"
      - "  8. Return jsonb {alert_id, status: 'acknowledged', acknowledged_by: v_actor_id, already_acknowledged: false}"
    sec_note_controls:
      - C1: Tenant isolation via Pattern C + manual casino_id binding in DEFINER body
      - C2: Role gate (pit_boss/admin) in rpc_acknowledge_alert
      - C3: Actor binding from app.actor_id (ADR-024 INV-8)
      - C4: Forward-only state machine (WHERE status='open')
      - C5: Server-side cooldown floor (GREATEST(5, config_value))
      - C6: DELETE denied via RLS policy (WS1)
      - C7: RPC-only mutation surface (REVOKE EXECUTE FROM PUBLIC)
    validation:
      - npm run db:types-local exits 0
      - Neither RPC accepts p_casino_id or p_actor_id parameters
      - REVOKE EXECUTE FROM PUBLIC verified on both RPCs
      - GRANT EXECUTE TO authenticated, service_role on both

  WS4:
    name: Service Layer Extensions
    description: >
      Extend existing services/shift-intelligence/ with alert DTOs, schemas,
      mappers, alerts.ts module, http fetchers, and key factories.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS2, WS3]
    outputs:
      - services/shift-intelligence/dtos.ts (extended)
      - services/shift-intelligence/schemas.ts (extended)
      - services/shift-intelligence/mappers.ts (extended)
      - services/shift-intelligence/alerts.ts (new)
      - services/shift-intelligence/http.ts (extended)
      - services/shift-intelligence/keys.ts (extended)
      - services/shift-intelligence/index.ts (extended)
    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: medium
    patterns:
      - "Pattern A: Contract-First DTOs (extends existing pattern in dtos.ts)"
      - Functional factory extension (add alert methods to ShiftIntelligenceServiceInterface)
      - Zod schemas in schemas.ts (not inline in route handlers) per ADR-013
      - React Query key factory with scope pattern
    implementation_hints:
      - "dtos.ts additions:"
      - "  - Add 'compute_failed' to ReadinessState union type"
      - "  - ShiftAlertDTO interface (id, tableId, tableLabel, metricType, gamingDay, status, severity, observedValue, baselineMedian, baselineMad, deviationScore, direction, message, createdAt, updatedAt, acknowledgment)"
      - "  - AlertAcknowledgmentDTO interface (acknowledgedBy, acknowledgedByName, notes, isFalsePositive, createdAt)"
      - "  - PersistAlertsInput interface (gaming_day?: string)"
      - "  - AcknowledgeAlertInput interface (alert_id: string, notes?: string, is_false_positive?: boolean)"
      - "  - PersistAlertsResultDTO interface (persistedCount, suppressedCount, gamingDay)"
      - "  - AcknowledgeAlertResultDTO interface (alertId, status, acknowledgedBy, alreadyAcknowledged)"
      - "  - AlertStatus type ('open' | 'acknowledged' | 'resolved') — 'resolved' is type-only for forward-compat, no UI/transition/test ships (DA P2-9)"
      - ""
      - "schemas.ts additions:"
      - "  - persistAlertsInputSchema: z.object({ gaming_day: z.string().date().optional() })"
      - "  - acknowledgeAlertSchema: z.object({ alert_id: z.string().uuid(), notes: z.string().max(1000).optional(), is_false_positive: z.boolean().optional() })"
      - "  - alertsQuerySchema: z.object({ gaming_day: z.string().date(), status: z.enum(['open', 'acknowledged']).optional() })"
      - ""
      - "mappers.ts additions:"
      - "  - mapShiftAlertRow: snake_case RPC/table row → ShiftAlertDTO"
      - "  - mapAcknowledgmentRow: snake_case row → AlertAcknowledgmentDTO"
      - "  - mapPersistResult: RPC jsonb → PersistAlertsResultDTO"
      - "  - mapAcknowledgeResult: RPC jsonb → AcknowledgeAlertResultDTO"
      - ""
      - "alerts.ts (new module):"
      - "  - persistAlerts(supabase, input?): calls rpc_persist_anomaly_alerts"
      - "  - acknowledgeAlert(supabase, input): calls rpc_acknowledge_alert"
      - "  - getAlerts(supabase, gamingDay, status?): SELECT from shift_alert LEFT JOIN alert_acknowledgment LEFT JOIN staff ON aa.acknowledged_by = staff.id to populate acknowledgedByName. Cross-context read justified by audit trail requirement (DA P1-4)"
      - ""
      - "keys.ts additions:"
      - "  - shiftAlerts scope: (gamingDay, status?) => [...ROOT, 'shift-alerts', { gamingDay, status }]"
      - ""
      - "[DA P1-3] Fix existing BASE constant: const BASE = '/api/v1/shift-intelligence' — corrects C-1 path mismatch before adding new fetchers"
      - ""
      - "http.ts additions:"
      - "  - fetchPersistAlerts(input?): POST /api/v1/shift-intelligence/persist-alerts"
      - "  - fetchAcknowledgeAlert(input): POST /api/v1/shift-intelligence/acknowledge-alert"
      - "  - fetchAlerts(gamingDay, status?): GET /api/v1/shift-intelligence/alerts"
      - ""
      - "index.ts additions:"
      - "  - Add persistAlerts, acknowledgeAlert, getAlerts to ShiftIntelligenceServiceInterface"
      - "  - Add to factory return object"
      - "  - Re-export new DTOs and types"
    validation:
      - npm run type-check exits 0
      - All new DTOs exported from index.ts
      - No as any casts
      - No ReturnType inference

  WS5:
    name: Route Handlers
    description: >
      Three API endpoints: persist-alerts (POST), acknowledge-alert (POST),
      alerts (GET). All under /api/v1/shift-intelligence/.
    executor: api-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS4]
    outputs:
      - app/api/v1/shift-intelligence/persist-alerts/route.ts
      - app/api/v1/shift-intelligence/acknowledge-alert/route.ts
      - app/api/v1/shift-intelligence/alerts/route.ts
    gate: lint
    estimated_complexity: medium
    patterns:
      - ServiceHttpResult response contract (lib/http/service-response.ts)
      - withServerAction middleware wrapper
      - createRequestContext for tracing
      - requireIdempotencyKey on POST endpoints
      - Zod validation from schemas.ts (not inline)
      - Error mapping per SRM Error Taxonomy
    implementation_hints:
      - "Follow compute-baselines/route.ts as structural precedent"
      - "[DA P1-2r3] PRD-056 §4.1 A8 uses /api/shift-intelligence/ paths (stale). Correct path is /api/v1/shift-intelligence/ per codebase convention. SRM v4.22.0 update must use /v1/ paths."
      - ""
      - "POST /api/v1/shift-intelligence/persist-alerts:"
      - "  - Input: persistAlertsInputSchema validation"
      - "  - Calls persistAlerts(mwCtx.supabase, input) via withServerAction"
      - "  - Returns PersistAlertsResultDTO"
      - "  - No role gate at route level (any authenticated staff can trigger)"
      - "  - requireIdempotencyKey"
      - ""
      - "POST /api/v1/shift-intelligence/acknowledge-alert:"
      - "  - Input: acknowledgeAlertSchema validation"
      - "  - Calls acknowledgeAlert(mwCtx.supabase, input) via withServerAction"
      - "  - Returns AcknowledgeAlertResultDTO"
      - "  - Role gate is in RPC (defense-in-depth), route can add pit_boss/admin check"
      - "  - requireIdempotencyKey"
      - "  - Error mapping: SHIFT_ACKNOWLEDGE_UNAUTHORIZED → 403, SHIFT_ALERT_NOT_FOUND → 404"
      - ""
      - "GET /api/v1/shift-intelligence/alerts:"
      - "  - Query params: gaming_day (required), status (optional)"
      - "  - alertsQuerySchema validation on searchParams"
      - "  - Calls getAlerts(mwCtx.supabase, gamingDay, status) via withServerAction"
      - "  - Returns { alerts: ShiftAlertDTO[] }"
      - "  - No idempotency key (GET)"
    error_codes:
      - SHIFT_ALERT_NOT_FOUND: 404
      - SHIFT_ACKNOWLEDGE_UNAUTHORIZED: 403
      - SHIFT_PERSIST_FAILED: 500
      - VALIDATION_ERROR: 400
    validation:
      - npm run type-check exits 0
      - npm run lint exits 0
      - All routes use ServiceHttpResult
      - All POST routes enforce idempotency key
      - export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic' on all routes

  WS6:
    name: Admin Alerts UI Wiring
    description: >
      Wire AnomalyAlertCard + BaselineCoverageBanner into /admin/alerts page.
      Replace in-memory dismiss with persistent acknowledge. Unified alert list
      with source badge (cash obs ephemeral + baseline persistent).
    executor: frontend-design-pt-2
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS5]
    outputs:
      - components/admin-alerts/alerts-page-client.tsx (modified)
      - components/shift-intelligence/anomaly-alert-card.tsx (modified — add acknowledge action)
      - components/admin-alerts/acknowledge-alert-dialog.tsx (new — notes + false-positive)
      - hooks/shift-intelligence/use-shift-alerts.ts (new — React Query hooks)
      - app/(dashboard)/admin/alerts/page.tsx (modified — add persist trigger on load)
    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: medium
    patterns:
      - "Mode A: Extending existing surface (/admin/alerts)"
      - PT-2 Visual DNA — border-2 Card, monospace headers, severity badges
      - useTransition for acknowledge action (React 19)
      - TanStack Query for alert data fetching
      - Source indicator badge (cash obs vs baseline)
      - Acknowledged alerts greyed out (opacity-50, border-muted)
    implementation_hints:
      - "[DA P2-6] useDismissedAlerts context REMAINS for ephemeral cash obs alerts. Persistent baseline alerts use useAcknowledgeAlert mutation. Both coexist in the unified alert list."
      - ""
      - "alerts-page-client.tsx: Add section for persistent baseline alerts alongside existing cash obs alerts"
      - "  - On mount: trigger fetchPersistAlerts() to sync anomaly results to shift_alert"
      - "  - Then fetch alerts via fetchAlerts(gamingDay) for persistent display"
      - "  - Cash obs alerts continue rendering ephemerally from existing RPC"
      - "  - Unified list with source badge: 'Baseline' (teal) vs 'Cash Obs' (amber)"
      - ""
      - "anomaly-alert-card.tsx: Add acknowledge button (pit_boss/admin only)"
      - "  - onClick opens AcknowledgeAlertDialog"
      - "  - Acknowledged state: greyed out card with 'Acknowledged by {name}' footer"
      - ""
      - "acknowledge-alert-dialog.tsx: Dialog with notes textarea + false-positive checkbox"
      - "  - Uses useTransition for submit"
      - "  - Calls fetchAcknowledgeAlert"
      - "  - Invalidates shiftAlerts query on success"
      - ""
      - "use-shift-alerts.ts: React Query hooks"
      - "  - useShiftAlerts(gamingDay, status?) — useQuery with shiftIntelligenceKeys.shiftAlerts"
      - "  - usePersistAlerts() — useMutation with fetchPersistAlerts"
      - "  - useAcknowledgeAlert() — useMutation with fetchAcknowledgeAlert + cache invalidation"
    surface_classification:
      note: "Existing surface extension — no full ADR-041 declaration required"
      rendering_delivery: "Hybrid (RSC page shell + client-side alert list)"
      data_aggregation: "Simple Query (shift_alert + LEFT JOIN alert_acknowledgment)"
    validation:
      - npm run type-check exits 0
      - Acknowledged alerts render as greyed out
      - Source badge distinguishes baseline vs cash obs
      - useTransition used for acknowledge (no manual loading state)

  WS7:
    name: Track A Integration + Contract Tests
    description: >
      Comprehensive test suite covering RLS isolation, role gates, state machine,
      cooldown, compute_failed, and route handler contracts.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS3, WS5]
    outputs:
      - services/shift-intelligence/__tests__/alerts.int.test.ts
      - services/shift-intelligence/__tests__/alerts-rpc-contract.test.ts
      - services/shift-intelligence/__tests__/alerts-http-contract.test.ts
    gate: test-pass
    estimated_complexity: high
    patterns:
      - "Integration tests (*.int.test.ts): real Supabase, two-casino fixtures"
      - "RPC contract tests: validate return shapes and error codes"
      - "HTTP contract tests: validate route handler responses"
      - "Test environment: node (not jsdom) per ADR-044 §4"
    implementation_hints:
      - "alerts.int.test.ts — Integration tests:"
      - "  - Two-casino isolation: Casino A cannot read/acknowledge Casino B alerts"
      - "  - Role gate denial: dealer/cashier cannot acknowledge"
      - "  - Backward transition rejected: acknowledged → open UPDATE fails"
      - "  - Concurrent acknowledgment atomicity: two calls → one transition, one audit record"
      - "  - Cooldown suppression: UPSERT skipped within cooldown window"
      - "  - Cooldown floor: cooldown_minutes < 5 treated as 5"
      - "  - compute_failed readiness: last_error IS NOT NULL → compute_failed state"
      - "  - DELETE rejection: DELETE on shift_alert and alert_acknowledgment denied"
      - "  - Direct mutation rejection: INSERT/UPDATE on tables without RPC denied"
      - ""
      - "alerts-rpc-contract.test.ts — RPC contract tests:"
      - "  - rpc_persist_anomaly_alerts returns { persisted_count, suppressed_count }"
      - "  - rpc_acknowledge_alert returns { alert_id, status, acknowledged_by, already_acknowledged }"
      - "  - Idempotent re-acknowledgment returns already_acknowledged: true"
      - ""
      - "alerts-http-contract.test.ts — Route handler tests:"
      - "  - POST persist-alerts returns correct shape"
      - "  - POST acknowledge-alert role gate denies unauthorized"
      - "  - GET alerts returns ShiftAlertDTO[] shape"
      - "  - 400 on invalid input (missing alert_id, invalid gaming_day)"
    validation:
      - All tests pass (npm test services/shift-intelligence/)
      - No test uses jsdom environment
      - Coverage >= 80% for alerts.ts module

  WS8:
    name: Context Enrichment RPC Amendment
    description: >
      Amend rpc_get_anomaly_alerts to add context enrichment columns:
      session_count, peak_deviation, recommended_action.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS7]
    outputs:
      - supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_add_alert_context_enrichment.sql
      - types/database.types.ts (regenerated)
      - services/shift-intelligence/dtos.ts (extended — add enrichment fields to AnomalyAlertDTO)
      - services/shift-intelligence/mappers.ts (extended — map new fields)
    gate: schema-validation
    estimated_complexity: low
    patterns:
      - CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION (idempotent amendment)
      - Nullable columns (backward-compatible return type extension)
    implementation_hints:
      - "Add to rpc_get_anomaly_alerts return type:"
      - "  - session_count int — count of active sessions on the table (from table_session)"
      - "  - peak_deviation numeric — max deviation_score across all metrics for this table"
      - "  - recommended_action text — 'investigate' | 'monitor' | 'acknowledge' based on severity"
      - "Update AnomalyAlertDTO: add sessionCount, peakDeviation, recommendedAction (all nullable)"
      - "Update mapAnomalyAlertRow: map new snake_case fields"
      - "[DA P2-3] CREATE OR REPLACE replaces the entire function body. WS8 output MUST include all WS2 readiness derivation (compute_failed CASE branch + bl CTE casino_id filter) in addition to enrichment columns. Coordinate with WS2 output."
      - "NOTIFY pgrst at end of migration"
    validation:
      - npm run db:types-local exits 0
      - Existing anomaly alert tests still pass (new fields are nullable)

  WS9:
    name: Dashboard + Settings UI Wiring
    description: >
      Wire AnomalyAlertCard into shift dashboard alerts-panel.tsx alongside
      existing cash obs alerts. Wire RecomputeBaselinesButton into /admin/settings.
    executor: frontend-design-pt-2
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS8, WS4]
    outputs:
      - components/shift-dashboard/alerts-panel.tsx (modified — add baseline alert section) # DA P2-4: fixed path from components/admin-alerts/
      - app/(protected)/shift-dashboard/page.tsx (modified — add alert prefetch if needed)
      - app/(dashboard)/admin/settings/page.tsx or settings client component (modified — add recompute button)
    gate: type-check
    estimated_complexity: low
    patterns:
      - "Mode A: Extending existing surfaces"
      - PT-2 Visual DNA — match existing alerts-panel.tsx Card/badge patterns
      - "30s refetch continues for live anomaly display (useAnomalyAlerts existing hook)"
      - "Persist is NOT piggybacked on refetch — explicit trigger only"
    implementation_hints:
      - "alerts-panel.tsx: Add baseline anomaly alerts section below existing cash obs alerts"
      - "  - Use AnomalyAlertCard (already styled in C-1)"
      - "  - Source badge to distinguish from cash obs"
      - "  - 30s refetch via existing useAnomalyAlerts continues for live ephemeral display"
      - ""
      - "EXPLICIT PERSIST TRIGGER ON DASHBOARD (PRD §6 Flow 1):"
      - "  - On shift dashboard mount, trigger usePersistAlerts() mutation (same as /admin/alerts)"
      - "  - This ensures shift_alert rows are current when the dashboard loads"
      - "  - The 30s refetch does NOT trigger persist — persist is on mount + manual 'Refresh Alerts' only"
      - "  - Add a 'Refresh Alerts' button alongside the existing dashboard controls"
      - ""
      - "RecomputeBaselinesButton: Wire into admin settings shift section"
      - "  - Button already exists in components/shift-intelligence/"
      - "  - Find appropriate settings section and add"
    validation:
      - npm run type-check exits 0
      - Both alert types display on shift dashboard
      - RecomputeBaselinesButton accessible from settings

  WS10:
    name: Alert Quality Service Layer + RPC
    description: >
      AlertQualityDTO, SECURITY INVOKER quality RPC (percentile_cont requires SQL),
      key factory scope, http wrapper. Suppression ratio deferred.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS7]
    gate: schema-validation
    estimated_complexity: low
    patterns:
      - "Pattern A: Contract-First DTO (computed aggregate, not table projection)"
      - "SECURITY INVOKER RPC (Pattern C RLS enforces casino scope — no DEFINER needed)"
      - "SET search_path = pg_catalog, public (ADR-046 pinned search_path)"
      - "percentile_cont(0.5) for median acknowledge latency"
    implementation_hints:
      - "AlertQualityDTO: totalAlerts, acknowledgedCount, falsePositiveCount, medianAcknowledgeLatencyMs, period"
      - "Suppression ratio NOT included — requires evaluation-cycle logging (deferred)"
      - ""
      - "IMPLEMENTATION APPROACH: Lightweight RPC (rpc_get_alert_quality). The query requires"
      - "  percentile_cont() which is not expressible via Supabase client .select(). A minimal"
      - "  SECURITY INVOKER RPC keeps the aggregation in SQL where it belongs. Pattern C RLS on"
      - "  shift_alert enforces casino scoping automatically (INVOKER inherits caller identity)."
      - ""
      - "rpc_get_alert_quality(p_start date, p_end date) RETURNS jsonb"
      - "LANGUAGE plpgsql STABLE SECURITY INVOKER"
      - "SET search_path = pg_catalog, public"
      - "AS $$"
      - "DECLARE"
      - "  v_result jsonb;"
      - "BEGIN"
      - "  SELECT jsonb_build_object("
      - "    'total_alerts', count(*),"
      - "    'acknowledged_count', count(*) FILTER (WHERE sa.status = 'acknowledged'),"
      - "    'false_positive_count', count(aa.id) FILTER (WHERE aa.is_false_positive),"
      - "    'median_acknowledge_latency_ms', percentile_cont(0.5) WITHIN GROUP"
      - "      (ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (aa.created_at - sa.created_at)) * 1000)"
      - "  ) INTO v_result"
      - "  FROM shift_alert sa"
      - "  LEFT JOIN alert_acknowledgment aa ON sa.id = aa.alert_id"
      - "  WHERE sa.gaming_day BETWEEN p_start AND p_end;  -- RLS enforces casino_id scope"
      - "  RETURN v_result;"
      - "END; $$;"
      - ""
      - "WS10 migration file: supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_create_alert_quality_rpc.sql"
      - "Service layer: alerts.ts getAlertQuality(supabase, startDate, endDate) calls supabase.rpc('rpc_get_alert_quality', { p_start, p_end })"
      - "Mapper: mapAlertQualityResult(jsonb) → AlertQualityDTO"
    outputs:
      - services/shift-intelligence/dtos.ts (extended — AlertQualityDTO)
      - services/shift-intelligence/alerts.ts (extended — getAlertQuality)
      - services/shift-intelligence/mappers.ts (extended — mapAlertQualityResult)
      - services/shift-intelligence/keys.ts (extended — alertQuality scope)
      - services/shift-intelligence/http.ts (extended — fetchAlertQuality)
      - services/shift-intelligence/index.ts (extended — add getAlertQuality)
      - supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_create_alert_quality_rpc.sql (new)
    validation:
      - npm run db:types-local exits 0
      - npm run type-check exits 0
      - AlertQualityDTO exported from index.ts
      - No suppressionRatio field (explicitly deferred)
      - RPC is SECURITY INVOKER (no DEFINER governance overhead)

  WS11:
    name: Grant Posture Audit
    description: >
      Validate REVOKE/GRANT posture on all new RPCs and tables.
      Verify SEC Note controls C6 and C7 are enforced.
    executor: rls-expert
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS7]
    outputs:
      - services/shift-intelligence/__tests__/grant-posture-audit.int.test.ts
    gate: test-pass
    estimated_complexity: low
    patterns:
      - SQL inspection queries against pg_catalog
      - SEC Note C6 (DELETE denial) + C7 (RPC-only mutation surface)
    implementation_hints:
      - "Verify via SQL queries:"
      - "  - SELECT has_function_privilege('public', 'rpc_persist_anomaly_alerts(date)', 'EXECUTE') = false"
      - "  - SELECT has_function_privilege('authenticated', 'rpc_persist_anomaly_alerts(date)', 'EXECUTE') = true"
      - "  - SELECT has_table_privilege('authenticated', 'shift_alert', 'INSERT') = false"
      - "  - SELECT has_table_privilege('authenticated', 'shift_alert', 'UPDATE') = false"
      - "  - SELECT has_table_privilege('authenticated', 'shift_alert', 'DELETE') = false"
      - "  - Same for alert_acknowledgment"
      - "  - Verify DELETE denial policy exists on both tables"
    validation:
      - All grant posture assertions pass
      - REVOKE EXECUTE FROM PUBLIC confirmed on both RPCs
      - No direct mutation grants on either table

execution_phases:
  - name: "Phase 1 — Schema Foundation"
    parallel: [WS1]
    gates: [schema-validation]

  - name: "Phase 2 — RPC Layer"
    parallel: [WS2, WS3]
    gates: [schema-validation]

  - name: "Phase 3 — Service Layer"
    parallel: [WS4]
    gates: [type-check]

  - name: "Phase 4 — Route Handlers"
    parallel: [WS5]
    gates: [type-check, lint]

  - name: "Phase 5 — Track A UI + Tests"
    parallel: [WS6, WS7]
    gates: [type-check, test-pass]

  - name: "Phase 6 — Track B Foundation"
    parallel: [WS8, WS10, WS11]
    gates: [schema-validation, type-check, test-pass]

  - name: "Phase 7 — Track B UI"
    parallel: [WS9]
    gates: [type-check, build]

gates:
  schema-validation:
    command: "npm run db:types-local"
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0, types regenerated successfully"

  type-check:
    command: "npm run type-check"
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0, no type errors"

  lint:
    command: "npm run lint"
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0"

  test-pass:
    command: "npm test services/shift-intelligence/ > /tmp/test-output.log 2>&1 && tail -20 /tmp/test-output.log"
    success_criteria: "All tests pass"

  build:
    command: "npm run build > /tmp/build-output.log 2>&1 && tail -5 /tmp/build-output.log"
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0"

external_dependencies:
  - prd: PRD-055
    service: ShiftIntelligenceService
    required_for: "table_metric_baseline, rpc_compute_rolling_baseline, rpc_get_anomaly_alerts, services/shift-intelligence/"

risks:
  - risk: "SRM not yet updated for shift_alert + alert_acknowledgment ownership"
    mitigation: "Include SRM v4.22.0 update as first deliverable of WS1"
  - risk: "rpc_persist_anomaly_alerts (DEFINER) calls rpc_get_anomaly_alerts (INVOKER) — INVOKER executes as postgres, bypassing RLS on table_metric_baseline"
    mitigation: "[DA P0-1 RESOLVED] WS2 adds explicit WHERE b.casino_id = v_casino_id to the bl CTE in rpc_get_anomaly_alerts, making the query tenant-safe regardless of execution context. Pattern C RLS alone is insufficient inside DEFINER calling chains."
  - risk: "Track B gates on Track A — no inter-track parallelism"
    mitigation: "Track A delivers GREEN (85%+) independently. Track B is P1, additive."
---

# EXECUTION-SPEC: PRD-056 — Alert Maturity (C-2/C-3)

## Overview

PRD-056 promotes anomaly alerts from ephemeral RPC results to a production-quality operational system. Three capabilities: (1) persistent alert storage with forward-only state machine and deduplication, (2) auditable acknowledgment with role-gated actor attribution, (3) alert quality telemetry. Organized in two tracks: Track A (C-2, P0) delivers schema + RPCs + UI wiring; Track B (C-3, P1) delivers context enrichment + dashboard wiring + telemetry.

## Scope

- **In Scope**: shift_alert + alert_acknowledgment tables, 2 new SECURITY DEFINER RPCs, 2 existing RPC amendments, 3 new route handlers, service layer extensions, /admin/alerts UI wiring, shift dashboard wiring, alert quality telemetry, grant posture audit
- **Out of Scope**: External notifications (banned by Pilot Containment), pg_cron, cash obs cutover, alert escalation chains, resolved state transition automation, ML anomaly detection

## Architecture Context

- **Bounded Context**: ShiftIntelligenceService (SRM v4.21.0 → v4.22.0)
- **Key ADRs**: ADR-046 (amendment §8 + §10), ADR-018 (DEFINER governance), ADR-024 (context derivation), ADR-030 (auth hardening)
- **SEC Note**: `docs/20-architecture/specs/alert-maturity/SEC_NOTE.md` — 7 threats, 7 controls
- **Predecessor**: PRD-055 / EXEC-055 (Phase C-1, commit `8402d59`)

## Workstream Details

### WS1: Schema + RLS Migration

**Purpose**: Create the persistent alert storage layer with casino-scoped RLS and RPC-only mutation posture.

**Deliverables**:
1. Single migration file creating `shift_alert`, `alert_acknowledgment` tables
2. ALTER `table_metric_baseline` to add `last_error` column
3. Pattern C hybrid RLS (SELECT only) on both new tables
4. DELETE denial policies on both tables
5. REVOKE direct INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE from authenticated role
6. SRM v4.22.0 update (add `shift_alert`, `alert_acknowledgment` to ShiftIntelligenceService)

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] `npm run db:types-local` succeeds
- [ ] Both tables have RLS enabled with Pattern C SELECT + DELETE denial
- [ ] No INSERT/UPDATE policies for authenticated role
- [ ] Composite UNIQUE on shift_alert(casino_id, table_id, metric_type, gaming_day)

### WS2: RPC Amendments (last_error + 5-state readiness)

**Purpose**: Extend existing RPCs to support compute_failed readiness state.

**Deliverables**:
1. Amend `rpc_compute_rolling_baseline` — populate/clear `last_error`
2. Amend `rpc_get_anomaly_alerts` — 5-state readiness derivation

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] `compute_failed` state returned when `last_error IS NOT NULL`
- [ ] `last_error` cleared on successful computation
- [ ] Existing tests pass (backward-compatible changes)

### WS3: New SECURITY DEFINER RPCs

**Purpose**: Create the mutation surface for alert persistence and acknowledgment with full ADR-024/030 compliance.

**Deliverables**:
1. `rpc_persist_anomaly_alerts` — UPSERT with dedup + cooldown
2. `rpc_acknowledge_alert` — role-gated atomic state transition
3. REVOKE/GRANT posture on both RPCs

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Neither RPC accepts `p_casino_id` or `p_actor_id` (ADR-024 INV-8)
- [ ] Both call `set_rls_context_from_staff()` as first operation
- [ ] Cooldown floor enforced at 5 minutes minimum
- [ ] Acknowledge transition is atomic (WHERE status='open')
- [ ] REVOKE EXECUTE FROM PUBLIC on both

### WS4: Service Layer Extensions

**Purpose**: Extend the TypeScript service layer with alert DTOs, schemas, mappers, and service functions.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] `npm run type-check` exits 0
- [ ] All new DTOs exported from service index
- [ ] New DTOs use Pattern A manual interfaces (with eslint-disable justification per existing dtos.ts convention)
- [ ] No `as any` or `ReturnType` inference

### WS5: Route Handlers

**Purpose**: Three API endpoints for alert persistence, acknowledgment, and retrieval.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] All routes use `ServiceHttpResult` contract
- [ ] POST routes enforce idempotency key
- [ ] `withServerAction` wraps all service calls
- [ ] Zod validation from schemas.ts

### WS6: Admin Alerts UI Wiring (Track A)

**Purpose**: Wire persistent alerts into the existing /admin/alerts page.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Unified alert list with source badge
- [ ] Persistent acknowledge replaces in-memory dismiss
- [ ] Acknowledged alerts greyed out
- [ ] `useTransition` for acknowledge action

### WS7: Track A Integration + Contract Tests

**Purpose**: Comprehensive test suite validating all Track A security and functional requirements.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] All DoD test items from PRD-056 §8 covered
- [ ] Two-casino isolation verified
- [ ] Role gate denial verified
- [ ] All tests pass

### WS8-WS11: Track B Workstreams

See YAML frontmatter for detailed specifications.

## Definition of Done

- [ ] All 11 workstreams complete
- [ ] All gates pass (schema-validation, type-check, lint, test-pass, build)
- [ ] No regressions in existing shift-intelligence tests
- [ ] SRM updated to v4.22.0
- [x] ADR-046 amendment §8 + §10 documented (pre-satisfied during RFC/scaffold phase — already in ADR file, DA P2-5)
- [ ] All SEC Note controls (C1-C7) validated by tests
- [ ] Accepted posture: rpc_persist_anomaly_alerts has no role gate (any authenticated staff can trigger) — documented in SEC Note deferred risks (DA P2-2)
- [ ] Wedge C scorecard: 85%+ after Track A, 92%+ after Track B
